home.title: Flame
home.description: A headless React component library for building accessible user interfaces.

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.subtitle: Learn how to integrate the Flame design system into your project.
docs.installDescription: Install Flame with your package manager of choice.
docs.peerDescription: Flame targets React 19 and uses Floating UI for positioning (Popover, Select, Tooltip). Install these alongside the package:
docs.usageDescription: Components follow a compound pattern with dot notation. They are headless — no default styles — so you wrap your own elements and style them freely.
docs.stylingDescription: Flame ships zero styles. Every component forwards your className and native props to the underlying element and exposes state through data-attributes (e.g. data-selected), so you can style with Tailwind, plain CSS, or any approach you prefer.

features.headless: Headless — fully functional with zero default styles
features.compound: Compound components — intuitive composition via dot notation
features.treeshake: Tree-shakeable — only bundle what you use
features.standards: Web standards — native <dialog> top layer and Floating UI positioning

accordion.title: Accordion
accordion.description: A headless accordion component with single and multi expand modes. Compose with Item, Trigger, and Content sub-components.

props.single: When true, only one item can be expanded at a time
props.itemInitialOpen: Initial expanded state of the item
props.triggerChildren: A clickable element or render function that receives the toggle handler. Receives aria-controls automatically
props.contentProps: Accepts native div element attributes; renders as <section /> with an auto-generated id

demo.whatIsFlameContent: Flame is a headless React 19 design system focused on composability and accessibility. It provides unstyled, fully functional components that you can style to match your design.
demo.gettingStartedContent: Install via pnpm add @flame/ui and import the components you need. Each component is fully tree-shakeable and ships with zero default styles.
demo.componentsContent: Flame includes Accordion, Dialog, Funnel, Popover, Select, Toast, and more. All components follow a compound component pattern with dot notation.

dialog.title: Dialog
dialog.description: A headless modal dialog component built on the native <dialog /> element. Supports outside-click dismissal and keep-mounted mode.

props.id: Custom id for the content (dialog) element. Auto-generated if omitted.
props.closeOutside: Close the dialog when clicking outside the content area
props.keepMounted: Keep content mounted in the DOM when dialog is closed
props.triggerChildren: A clickable element or render function that receives the open handler. Receives aria-haspopup automatically
props.contentProps: Native dialog element attributes (excluding onClose). Receives an auto-generated id
props.closerChildren: A clickable element or render function that receives the close handler

demo.welcomeDescription: This is a headless dialog built on the native <dialog /> element. It supports outside-click dismissal and keep-mounted mode.

drawer.title: Drawer
drawer.description: A headless drawer component that slides in from any edge of the viewport. Built on the native <dialog> top layer for focus trapping, ESC key handling, and nested-drawer stacking. Compose with Trigger, Content, and Closer sub-components.

props.placement: Edge of the viewport the drawer slides in from
props.id: Custom id for the content element. Auto-generated if omitted
props.closeOutside: Close the drawer when clicking outside the content area (backdrop)
props.keepMounted: Keep content mounted in the DOM when the drawer is closed
props.animation: Slide animation config (Web Animations API). Defaults to a placement-based slide; pass false to disable. Respects prefers-reduced-motion.
props.triggerChildren: A clickable element or render function that receives the open handler
props.contentProps: Native dialog element attributes (excluding onClose/onClick). Rendered into the top layer via showModal with focus trapping
props.closerChildren: A clickable element or render function that receives the close handler

demo.title: Drawer Panel
demo.description: This drawer is built on the native <dialog> top layer with focus trapping, ESC key handling, and nested stacking.

funnel.title: Funnel
funnel.description: A step-by-step wizard component. Compose with Step, Next, and Prev sub-components to build multi-step flows.

props.stepChildren: Render function that receives a jump callback to navigate to a specific step
props.nextChildren: A clickable element or render function that receives the next handler. Receives aria-disabled at the last step
props.prevChildren: A clickable element or render function that receives the prev handler. Receives aria-disabled at the first step

demo.enterInfoDescription: Fill in your details to get started.

popover.title: Popover
popover.description: A headless popover component powered by Floating UI. Supports portal rendering, focus trapping, dismiss handling, and CSS transitions out of the box.

props.dismiss: Configure dismiss behavior (outside click, escape key)
props.onOpen: Called when the popover opens (uncontrolled mode)
props.onClose: Called when the popover closes (uncontrolled mode)
props.open: Controlled mode: externally controlled open state (use with onOpenChange)
props.onOpenChange: Controlled mode: called when the open state should change
props.options: Options forwarded to Floating UI useFloating (placement, middleware, strategy, etc.)
props.triggerChildren: A clickable element that toggles the popover
props.contentChildren: Content to display or a render function with floating context
props.portal: Render content in a portal (passed to Popover.Content)

demo.popoverDescription: This is a headless popover powered by Floating UI. It supports portal rendering, focus trapping, and CSS transitions.

select.title: Select
select.description: A headless select/dropdown component powered by Floating UI. Supports controlled value, portal rendering, and CSS transitions.

props.onChange: Callback when an option is selected
props.dismiss: Configure dismiss behavior (outside click, escape key)
props.onOpen: Called when the options list opens (uncontrolled mode)
props.onClose: Called when the options list closes (uncontrolled mode)
props.open: Controlled mode: externally controlled open state (use with onOpenChange)
props.onOpenChange: Controlled mode: called when the open state should change
props.options: Options forwarded to Floating UI useFloating (placement, middleware, strategy, etc.)
props.triggerChildren: A clickable element that toggles the options list
props.optionsChildren: Option elements or a render function with floating context
props.portal: Render options in a portal (passed to Select.Options)
props.optionsProps: All native div element attributes. Renders with role="listbox"
props.optionProps: All native div element attributes. Renders with role="option" and aria-selected

toast.title: Toast
toast.description: An imperative toast notification system. Place a Toaster container once, then call toast() anywhere to show notifications with auto-dismiss.

props.placement: Position of the toast container on screen
props.animation: Animation for toast open/close. Pass false to disable
props.toastProps: All native div element attributes. Receives role="status" and aria-live="polite". Auto-links Toast.Title and Toast.Description via aria-labelledby/aria-describedby, so include both.
props.titleProps: All native div element attributes. Linked to parent Toast via aria-labelledby
props.descriptionProps: All native div element attributes. Linked to parent Toast via aria-describedby

demo.successDescription: Your changes have been saved successfully.
demo.infoDescription: This notification will dismiss in 5 seconds.

tabs.title: Tabs
tabs.description: A headless tab component for switching between content panels. Compose with Item sub-components.

props.initialTab: The value of the initially selected tab
props.onChange: Callback when a tab is selected. Receives the selected tab's value
props.rootProps: All native div element attributes. Renders with role="tablist"
props.itemValue: Unique value used to identify the tab
props.itemProps: All native button element attributes. Receives role="tab", aria-selected, and data-selected automatically

demo.overviewContent: Flame is a headless React 19 design system focused on composability and accessibility.
demo.usageContent: Install via pnpm add @flame/ui and import the components you need.
demo.apiContent: All components follow a compound component pattern with dot notation.

tooltip.title: Tooltip
tooltip.description: A headless tooltip component powered by Floating UI. Shows contextual content on hover and focus, with placement, offset, dismiss handling, and CSS transitions out of the box.

props.enabled: Whether the tooltip is enabled. When false, the tooltip never opens
props.placement: Tooltip placement (top, bottom, left, right and variants)
props.offset: Pixel gap between the trigger and the tooltip
props.flip: Whether and how to flip to the opposite side when there is not enough space
props.shift: Options for shifting along the axis to stay within the viewport
props.delay: Hover open/close delay in ms or a { open, close } object
props.dismiss: Configure dismiss behavior (outside click, escape key)
props.onOpen: Called when the tooltip opens (uncontrolled mode)
props.onClose: Called when the tooltip closes (uncontrolled mode)
props.open: Controlled mode: externally controlled open state (use with onOpenChange)
props.onOpenChange: Controlled mode: called when the open state should change
props.options: Options forwarded to Floating UI useFloating (placement, middleware, strategy, etc.)
props.triggerChildren: The element that acts as the tooltip trigger
props.contentChildren: Tooltip content to display or a render function with floating context
